3298.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Yusuf narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Layth, who said: Tawus said to me: "Whatever you have learned, learn it for yourself, for people have lost their trustworthiness."

3299.iwh_en: Sulayman ibn Harb narrated to us, from 'Umarah ibn Mihran, from al-Hasan, who said: "I knew the people when a devout worshipper, if he worshipped, was not recognized by his speech, but was recognized by his deeds — that is beneficial knowledge."

3300.iwh_en: Abu al-Mughirah narrated to us, al-Awza'i narrated to us, from Hassan, from Abu Kabshah, who said: I heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (may Allah be pleased with him) say: I heard the Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) say: "Convey from me, even a single verse, and narrate from the Children of Israel — there is no harm in it. And whoever lies about me deliberately, let him take his seat in the Fire."

3301.iwh_en: 'Ali ibn Hujr al-Sa'di narrated to us, Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, al-'Awwam ibn Hawshab Abu 'Isa al-Shaybani narrated to us, al-Qasim ibn 'Awf al-Shaybani narrated to us, from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) commanded us "that none should overcome us in three things: commanding what is right, forbidding what is wrong, and teaching the people the Sunan."

3302.iwh_en: Abu al-Mughirah narrated to us, Safwan narrated to us, Sulaym ibn 'Amir narrated to me, who said: When we sat with Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him), he would bring us matters of great importance from the hadith and say: "Listen, understand, and convey from us what you hear." Sulaym said: "[He was] like one who bears witness to what he knew."

3303.iwh_en: 'Abd al-Wahhab ibn Sa'id narrated to us, Shu'ayb — who is Ibn Ishaq — narrated to us, al-Awza'i narrated to us, Abu Kathir narrated to me, my father narrated to me, who said: I came to Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) while he was sitting by the middle Jamrah. The people had gathered around him seeking religious rulings. A man came and stood over him and said: "Were you not forbidden from giving fatwas?" He raised his head toward him and said: "Are you a watchman over me? If you were to place the sword on this" — and he gestured to the back of his neck — "and I thought I could convey a word I heard from the Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) before you carried it out, I would convey it."

3304.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn 'Isa narrated to us, 'Abbad — who is Ibn al-'Awwam — narrated to us, from 'Awf, from Abu al-'Aliyah, who said: I asked Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with both of them) about something, and he said: "O Abu al-'Aliyah, do you want to become a mufti?" I said: "No, but I fear you may pass away and we remain." He said: "Abu al-'Aliyah has spoken the truth."

3305.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn 'Isa narrated to us, 'Abbad narrated to us, from Husayn, from Ibrahim, who said: "'Abidah used to come to 'Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) every Thursday and ask him about things he had missed. Most of what was preserved from 'Abdullah was what 'Abidah asked him about."

3306.iwh_en: Al-Hakam ibn al-Mubarak narrated to us, Ghassan — who is Ibn Mudar — narrated to us, from Sa'id ibn Yazid, who said: I heard 'Ikrimah say: "Why do you not ask me? Have you gone bankrupt?"

3307.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Hatim al-Muktib narrated to us, 'Amir ibn Salih narrated to us, Yunus narrated to us, from Ibn Shihab, who said: "Knowledge is like treasuries, and questions are what open them."

3308.iwh_en: Ibrahim ibn Ishaq narrated to us, from Jarir, who said: Ibrahim said: "Whoever has a thin face, his knowledge will be thin." Waki' narrated to us, from his father, from al-Sha'bi, who said: "Whoever has a thin face, his knowledge will be thin." And from Damrah, from Hafs ibn 'Umar, who said: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab said: "Whoever has a thin face, his knowledge will be thin." [i.e., whoever lacks boldness to ask and learn will have scanty knowledge]

3309.iwh_en: Ibrahim ibn Ishaq narrated to us, from Jarir, from a man, from Mujahid, who said: "One who is bashful and arrogant will not learn."

3310.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Khalaf narrated to us, Anas ibn 'Iyad narrated to us, from Hisham ibn 'Urwah, from his father, that he would gather his children and say: "O my sons, learn! For if you are now the young of a people, perhaps you will become the elders of others. And how ugly it is for an old man to be asked while having no knowledge."

3311.iwh_en: Abu al-Nu'man narrated to us, Hammad ibn Zayd narrated to us, from al-Zubayr ibn al-Khirrit, from 'Ikrimah, who said: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) "would put shackles on my feet and teach me the Quran and the Sunan."

3312.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Humayd narrated to us, Yahya ibn al-Duraysis narrated to us, who said: I heard Sufyan say: "Whoever seeks leadership quickly harms a great deal of knowledge. But whoever does not seek leadership, keeps seeking and seeking until he attains it."

3313.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Yusuf narrated to us, from Sufyan, from al-A'mash, from Salih ibn Khabbab, from Husayn ibn 'Uqbah, from Salman (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: "Knowledge that is not spoken is like a treasure that is not spent."

3314.iwh_en: Ahmad ibn 'Abdullah narrated to us, Abu Shihab narrated to us, Ibrahim narrated to me, from Abu 'Iyad, from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) said: "The example of knowledge that is not benefited from is like the example of a treasure that is not spent in the way of Allah."

3315.iwh_en: Ya'la narrated to us, Muhammad — who is Ibn Ishaq — narrated to us, from Musa ibn Yasar his uncle, who said: It reached me that Salman (may Allah be pleased with him) wrote to Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him): "Indeed knowledge is like springs that people visit; this one and that one draw from it, and Allah benefits many through it. A wisdom that is not spoken is like a body with no soul in it. Knowledge that is not brought out is like a treasure that is not spent. The example of the scholar is like..."

3316.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn al-Salt narrated to us, Mansur ibn Abi al-Aswad narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq al-Shaybani, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: "Three things follow a man after his death: a running charity he leaves behind, the prayers of his child for him, and knowledge he spread that is acted upon after him."

3317.iwh_en: Musa ibn Isma'il narrated to us, Isma'il ibn Ja'far al-Madani narrated to me, from al-'Ala' ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him), from the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him), who said: "When a person dies, his deeds are cut off except for three: beneficial knowledge, a running charity, or a righteous child who supplicates for him."

3318.iwh_en: 'Ubayd ibn Ya'ish narrated to us, Yunus narrated to us, from Salih ibn Rustum al-Muzani, from al-Hasan, from Abu Musa (may Allah be pleased with him), that he said when he arrived in Basra: "'Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) sent me to you to teach you the Book of your Lord and your Sunnah, and to purify your roads."

3319.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Humayd narrated to us, Muhammad ibn al-Mu'alla narrated to us, Ziyad ibn Khaythamah narrated to us, from Abu Dawud, from 'Abdullah ibn Sakhbarah, from Sakhbarah (may Allah be pleased with him), from the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him), who said: "Whoever seeks knowledge, it will be an expiation for what has passed."

3320.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn 'Isa narrated to us, Hammad ibn Zayd narrated to us, from Ayyub, from Abu Qilabah, who said: "I stayed in Madinah for three days with no need except that I had fulfilled it, except that a man whom they were expecting would narrate a hadith. So I stayed until he arrived and I asked him."

3321.iwh_en: Al-Hakam ibn al-Mubarak narrated to us, al-Walid ibn Muslim narrated to us, from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid ibn Jabir, who said: I heard Busr ibn 'Ubayd Allah say: "I used to ride to a region among the regions for a single hadith just to hear it."

3322.iwh_en: 'Amr ibn Zurarah narrated to us, Abu Qatan 'Amr ibn al-Haytham narrated to us, from Abu Khaldah, from Abu al-'Aliyah, who said: "We used to hear narrations in Basra from the Companions of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him), but we were not satisfied until we rode to Madinah and heard them directly from their mouths."

3323.iwh_en: Nu'aym ibn Hammad narrated to us, Baqiyyah narrated to us, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman al-Qushayri, who said: The Prophet Dawud (peace be upon him) said: "Tell the seeker of knowledge to take a staff of iron and sandals of iron, and seek knowledge until the staff breaks and the sandals wear out."

3324.iwh_en: Makhlad ibn Malik narrated to us, Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Umawi narrated to us, al-Hajjaj narrated to us, from Husayn ibn 'Abd al-Rahman from the family of Sa'd ibn Mu'adh, who said: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with both of them) said: "I sought knowledge and found most of it with the Ansar. I would come to a man and be told he was asleep, so I would fold my garment as a pillow and lie down until he came out at Dhuhr. He would say: 'When did you get here, cousin of the Messenger of Allah?' I would say: 'A long time ago.' He would say: 'How badly you did — why did you not tell me?' I would say..."

3325.iwh_en: Ahmad ibn 'Abdullah ibn Yunus narrated to us, Abu Bakr narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn 'Amr, from Abu Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: "Most of the hadith of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) was found with this tribe of the Ansar. By Allah, I used to come to a man among them and be told he was sleeping. If I wished, he could be woken for me, but I would leave him until he came out so that his narrating to me would feel good."

3326.iwh_en: Abu Ma'mar Isma'il ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, from Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah, from al-Zuhri, from Abu Salamah, who said: "If I had been gentle with Ibn 'Abbas, I would have gained much knowledge from him."

3327.iwh_en: Bishr ibn al-Hakam narrated to us, 'Abd al-Razzaq narrated to us, Ma'mar narrated to us, from al-Zuhri, who said: "I used to come to the door of 'Urwah and sit by the door. And though I could have entered if I wished, I would sit out of reverence for him."

3328.iwh_en: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, Jarir ibn Hazim narrated to us, from Ya'la ibn Hakim, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: "When the Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) passed away, I said to a man from the Ansar: 'Come, let us ask the Companions of the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him), for today they are many.' He said: 'How strange, O Ibn 'Abbas! Do you think the people need you while among them are...?' [Ibn 'Abbas continued:] I would hear of a hadith from a man and come to him while he was resting. I would fold my garment as a pillow at his door, and the wind would blow dust onto my face. He would come out and see me and say: 'O cousin of the Messenger of Allah, what brings you here? Why did you not send for me and I would have come to you?' I would say: 'No, I am more deserving to come to you.' And I would ask him about the hadith. He said: The man lived to see me and the people had gathered around me..."

3329.iwh_en: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, al-Jurayri narrated to us, from 'Abdullah ibn Buraydah, that a man from the Companions of the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) traveled to Fadalah ibn 'Ubayd (may Allah be pleased with him) in Egypt and found him attending to his she-camel. He said: "Welcome!" The visitor said: "I did not come to visit you, but rather you and I both heard a hadith from the Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him), and I hoped you might have knowledge of it." He said: "It was such and such."

3330.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Sa'id narrated to us, 'Abd al-Salam ibn Harb narrated to us, from 'Abd al-A'la, from al-Hasan, that he entered the market and was bargaining with a man over a garment. The man said: "It is yours for such-and-such, and by Allah, if it were anyone other than you I would not give it for this." Al-Hasan said: "Have you done this?" — and he was never seen buying or selling in the market again until he met Allah, Exalted be He.

3331.iwh_en: Al-Haytham ibn Jamil narrated to us, from Husam, from Abu Ma'shar, from Ibrahim, that he "would not buy from anyone who knew him."

3332.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Sa'id narrated to us, 'Abd al-Salam narrated to us, from 'Abdullah ibn al-Walid al-Muzani, from 'Ubayd ibn al-Hasan, who said: Mus'ab ibn al-Zubayr distributed wealth among the Quran reciters of Kufa when the month of Ramadan entered. He sent two thousand dirhams to 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ma'qil and said: "Use this during your month." 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ma'qil sent it back and said: "We did not recite the Quran for this."

3333.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Khalaf narrated to us, Anas ibn 'Iyad narrated to us, 'Ubayd Allah ibn 'Umar narrated to me, that 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah's pleasure be upon him) said to 'Abdullah ibn Salam (may Allah be pleased with him): "Who are the masters of knowledge?" He said: "Those who act on what they know." He said: "And what expels knowledge from the hearts of men?" He said: "Greed."

3334.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad narrated to us, Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us, from Zayd, from 'Ata', who said: "Nothing combines with anything more beautifully than forbearance combined with knowledge."

3335.iwh_en: 'Affan narrated to us, Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, 'Asim al-Ahwal narrated to us, from 'Amir al-Sha'bi, who said: "The adornment of knowledge is the forbearance of its people."

3336.iwh_en: Ya'qub ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, Zam'ah ibn Salih narrated to us, from Salamah ibn Wahram, from Tawus, who said: "Knowledge has never been carried in a better vessel than forbearance."

3337.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Humayd narrated to us, Jarir narrated to us, from Ibn Shubrumah, from al-Sha'bi, who said: "The adornment of knowledge is the forbearance of its people."

3338.iwh_en: Al-Hakam ibn al-Mubarak narrated to us, Mutarrif ibn Mazin narrated to us, from Ya'la ibn Miqsam, from Wahb ibn Munabbih, who said: "Wisdom dwells in the calm, tranquil heart."

3339.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Ahmad narrated to us, who said: I heard Sufyan say: 'Ubayd Allah said: "You have tarnished knowledge and removed its light. If 'Umar — may Allah's pleasure be upon him — had found us and you, he would have beaten us."

3340.iwh_en: Shihab ibn 'Abbad narrated to us, Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us, from Umayy al-Muradi, who said: 'Ali (may Allah's pleasure be upon him) said: "Learn knowledge. When you have learned it, hold it close and do not mix it with laughter or play, lest hearts spit it out."

3341.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Humayd narrated to us, Jarir narrated to us, from al-Fudayl ibn Ghazwan, from 'Ali ibn Husayn (may Allah have mercy on him), who said: "Whoever laughs one laugh spits out some of his knowledge."

3342.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Yusuf narrated to us, from Sufyan, that 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said to Ka'b: "Who are the masters of knowledge?" He said: "Those who act on what they know." He said: "And what expels knowledge from the hearts of scholars?" He said: "Greed."

3343.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Humayd narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Bishr narrated to us, 'Abdullah ibn al-Walid narrated to us, from 'Umar ibn Ayyub, from Abu Iyas, who said: I was staying with 'Amr ibn al-Nu'man when a messenger of Mus'ab ibn al-Zubayr came to him at the arrival of Ramadan with two thousand dirhams. He said: "The amir sends you greetings, and says: we have not left any noble reciter without sending him something from us. Use these toward your expenses for this month." He said: "Give my greetings to the amir and tell him: 'By Allah, we did not recite the Quran wanting the world and its dirhams.'"

3344.iwh_en: Asad ibn Musa narrated to us, Mu'awiyah narrated to us, al-Hasan ibn Jabir narrated to us, from al-Miqdam ibn Ma'di Karib al-Kindi (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) "forbade certain things on the Day of Khaybar: the domestic donkey and other things." Then he said: "Soon a man will be reclining on his couch, being told of my hadith and he will say: 'Between us and you is the Book of Allah — what we find in it of the permitted, we deem permitted, and what we find in it of the forbidden, we deem forbidden.' Know that what the Messenger of Allah has forbidden is like what Allah has forbidden."

3345.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq al-Fazari, from al-Awza'i, from Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, who said: "The Sunnah judges over the Quran, but the Quran does not judge over the Sunnah." [i.e., the Sunnah specifies and elaborates what is general in the Quran]

3346.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Kathir narrated to us, from al-Awza'i, from Hassan, who said: "Jibril used to descend upon the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him) with the Sunnah just as he descended upon him with the Quran."

3347.iwh_en: Muhammad ibn Kathir narrated to us, from al-Awza'i, from Makhul, who said: "The Sunnah is of two types: a Sunnah whose observance is an obligation and whose abandonment is disbelief; and a Sunnah whose observance is a virtue and whose abandonment entails no harm."
